resume:
Daniel Osei
Research Assistant — Computational Biology

Objective: A junior research scientist role in genomics or ML-for-bio.

Experience
Helix Genomics Lab (2023-present) — Research Assistant
- Summary line: Led the lab's single-cell RNA-seq analysis pipeline end to end and mentored two junior students.
- Detail bullet: Assisted a postdoc with parts of the single-cell analysis; contributed scripts under her direction.
- Ran QC and clustering on roughly 40 datasets (one section); elsewhere the resume states 'contributed to ~12 datasets'.
- First author on a methods paper (header); listed as third author in the publications section.

University Research Project (2022)
- Built a small variant-calling benchmark for a capstone

Education
BSc Bioinformatics (2023)

Skills
Python, R, scanpy, Seurat, variant calling, statistics
jd: Helix Genomics is hiring a Junior Research Scientist for our computational biology team. Reporting to a senior scientist, you will run single-cell RNA-seq analyses, maintain reproducible pipelines, and contribute to methods papers. We expect candidates to describe their own contributions precisely — exactly which steps they personally performed versus what they assisted with — and to reconcile any discrepancies between how their role is summarized and the underlying detail. We value intellectual honesty about authorship and scope, tight and specific answers, and reproducible, well-documented analysis work.

history.q: Tell me about your role on the single-cell pipeline at Helix — what did you personally do?
Your resume summary says you 'led the pipeline end to end,' but a detail line says you assisted a postdoc under her direction. Which is accurate?

candidate_last_answer: The detail line is accurate; the summary overstates it and I should fix that. Concretely: the postdoc, Dr. Lin, owned the pipeline design and the clustering parameters. I personally wrote the QC and doublet-removal scripts in scanpy, ran them on 12 of the datasets, and documented the parameters in our shared repo. I did not lead it end to end, and on the methods paper I'm third author, not first — the header on my resume is a typo I'll correct.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: ran them on 12 of the datasets

expected-yield: a precise, verifiable boundary of his own contribution — which specific QC and doublet-removal steps he authored versus what Dr. Lin designed, and how the 12-dataset figure reconciles with the resume's '~40 datasets' claim — confirming the scope of his honest correction
